title: Colour of complexes
summary: 9701 A Level — d–d transitions, ligand field splitting, and complementary colour in transition metal complexes.
learningObjectives: Explain why transition metal complexes are coloured.
Describe d orbital splitting in an octahedral field.
Relate ligand identity to colour changes.
Identify colourless d⁰ and d¹⁰ ions.

simpleExplanation.title: Why complexes are coloured
simpleExplanation.summary: Ligands split d orbitals. d–d electron transitions absorb visible light; the complementary colour is observed.
steps: Different ligands → different ΔE → different colour.
Sc³⁺, Zn²⁺ colourless — d⁰ or d¹⁰.
Cu²⁺ aqua blue; with Cl⁻ ligands colour changes.

sections.content: **Colour of complexes** (28.3) links **electronic structure** to **observation**. Explain **absorption** and **complementary colour**.
